concept: Sealed negative-pressure (subatmospheric) water/low-GWP two-phase cold-plate loop for >2 kW/chip Chinese AI racks - leak-safe by physics (air leaks in, coolant never leaks out), PFAS-free by design
named_buyer_examples: Envicool (Shenzhen CDU/thermal vendor, one of 7 vendors building Google Deschutes-class CDUs, L14-043)
Alibaba-class hyperscale operators already running HVDC datacenters (L02-048, L02-049)
Chinese colocation operators facing GB 40879 PUE caps (L14-035)
painful_job: Chinese operators must hit PUE <=1.25/1.2 caps (GB 40879 revision, L14-035) and 'high-efficiency cooling' policy direction (Guo-Neng-Fa-Ke-Ji [2026] No.34, L14-036 - NB: primary text has NO 100%-liquid-cooling mandate) while chip TDP heads to 3-5 kW and 800-1,000 W/cm2 (L14-010), beyond single-phase cold-plate margins; two-phase alternatives historically depended on discontinued PFAS dielectric fluids (two-phase literature L14-001/002/003/029).
product: A rack-level two-phase loop that runs water or low-GWP fluid below atmospheric pressure through microchannel evaporator cold plates (mechanism: saturation temperature tuned via vacuum setpoint; latent heat absorbs >2 kW/chip at near-isothermal junction; any seal failure leaks air inward, detected by vacuum decay, instead of coolant onto boards). Ships as evaporator plates + manifold + compact vacuum-condenser CDU compatible with T/CIEP 0263-2025 interfaces (L14-039).
frontier_vision: Become the default leak-safe two-phase thermal architecture for East-Asian AI infrastructure, then extend the same negative-pressure loop to power electronics, EV fast-charge cabinets and aerospace power-dense converters.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: university/lab-hosted loop testbed; replicate ITRI-class low-pressure results (L14-053 reports 2.4 kW/chip) on a 1 kW simulated die; file 2-3 core patents (vacuum-setpoint control, inward-leak detection, manifold blind-mate). 2027-2028: joint evaluation agreements (pre-company MOUs) with one Chinese thermal vendor and one TW/JP ODM lab; publish peer-reviewed validation. 2028-2029: 20-50 kW rack demonstrator with a partner lab in HK/Shenzhen; customer discovery with 10+ Chinese operators/ODM thermal teams on GB 40879 compliance economics; qualify supply chain for pumps/vacuum hardware.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Single-phase cold plates commoditize by 2030 in China, but >2 kW/chip parts push past single-phase margins exactly as hub-node PUE caps tighten; two-phase is still non-converged in 2026 (four competing architectures, L12-021-class CPO thermal debate; L14-001/002/003) so a 2030 entrant with a leak-safe niche is early, not late.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) GB 40879 revised PUE caps (<=1.25 new large DCs, <=1.2 national hub nodes) enforced through 2030-2034 buildout (L14-035); (b) East-Data-West-Computing (Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan) hub-node datacenter parks explicitly referenced as active buildout in accepted CN sources (L02-049); (c) national AI-energy action plan cooling-efficiency line (L14-036).
competition_outlook_2030: Envicool and other Chinese CDU incumbents (7+ vendors already building to Deschutes spec, L14-043) will dominate single-phase; ITRI-fed Taiwan ODM two-phase lines (L14-053) are the closest technical rival; expect at least one Chinese two-phase startup. Differentiator must be the negative-pressure leak-safety mechanism plus fluid-supply independence.
timing_window_risk: Foreign-founder access: direct sales to Chinese hyperscalers unrealistic; route is IP licensing/joint development with a Chinese thermal vendor (Envicool-class) or ODM design-in via HK entity. Risk that Chinese incumbents productize two-phase in-house before 2030; mitigated by patent position and the documented non-convergence of two-phase architectures.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no Chinese or Taiwanese ODM/thermal-vendor paid pilot (>=100 kW deployment) by mid-2034.
extreme_edge: Same loop physics scales to 1,000 W/cm2-class WBG power modules and directed-energy thermal buses.
first_experiment: 2027-Q2: side-by-side test of negative-pressure water two-phase plate vs best commercial single-phase plate on a 1-2 kW thermal test die; success = >=30% lower junction-to-coolant resistance at equal pump power plus demonstrated inward-leak fail-safe.
merge_import_notes: 2026-07-13: no content imported. C-04 (FIX) adopts B-01's negative-pressure architecture as its primary variant for the US+CN dual play; B-01 remains the CN-primary canonical with the licensing/ODM access route. Cluster duplicate C-04 is retained as a separate FIX seed per adjudication.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (PROMOTE): Best China-primary seed and only mechanism-level original in thermal family (negative-pressure leak-safe two-phase, PFAS-free by design); correctly rejects the debunked 100%-liquid-mandate claim; access via licensing/ODM honestly planned.

concept: Liquid-cooling qualification and reliability test platform: standardized CDU/cold-plate/coolant test benches and accelerated-aging protocols aligned to T/CIEP 0263-2025 and hyperscaler specs
named_buyer_examples: Envicool-class Chinese CDU/cold-plate vendors needing third-party-grade verification (L14-043)
Chinese datacenter operators doing acceptance testing against GB 40879 procurement terms (L14-035)
Chinese certification/inspection labs adopting T/CIEP 0263-2025 (L14-039)
painful_job: Buyer-facing thermal specs for the highest-value target (GB200-class racks) are inconsistent even in vendor materials (documented TDP/flow contradictions, L14-044), and China now has a dedicated liquid-cooling technical specification (T/CIEP 0263-2025, L14-039) plus PUE caps (L14-035) but no widely available independent bench infrastructure to verify approach-temperature, pressure-drop, corrosion, clogging and blind-mate reliability claims (Deschutes spec demands 3C approach / ~80 PSI, L14-043).
product: A modular test platform: (1) 50-250 kW thermohydraulic CDU test cart with calibrated heat-load banks and transient GPU-load emulation; (2) cold-plate metrology cell (thermal resistance mapping, two-phase-capable); (3) coolant-chemistry accelerated corrosion/biofouling rigs; (4) automated report generation against T/CIEP 0263-2025 clauses. Mechanism: traceable calorimetry plus scripted transient profiles reproducing documented rack load steps.
frontier_vision: Become the JEDEC-bench equivalent for liquid-cooled computing across Asia - the reference lab hardware every vendor and buyer cites in contracts, expanding into two-phase, immersion, and vehicle/power-electronics cooling qualification.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: build one reference bench in a university/3rd-party lab; run a published round-robin comparing 3+ commercial cold plates against public specs (L14-043/044 discrepancies make this publishable and marketing-grade). 2027-2028: draft test-method proposals into Chinese standards ecosystem via partner lab (CIEP-adjacent); IP on transient-load emulation and in-situ corrosion sensing. 2028-2029: paid pilot testing-as-research for 2-3 Chinese vendors; spec the productized cart line.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Standards (2025-2026 vintage: L14-039, L14-035) precede enforcement and mass procurement; the verification market matures 3-5 years after the standard, landing exactly at a 2030 company start; test equipment resists commoditization because credibility and traceability accumulate.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: GB 40879 PUE enforcement plus T/CIEP 0263-2025 adoption in hub-node procurement contracts through the 15th Five-Year-Plan datacenter buildout (L14-035, L14-039, L02-049); every new liquid-cooled hub-node park needs acceptance testing.
competition_outlook_2030: Chroma ATE-class test incumbents (L11-043) could extend from battery/server test into thermal benches; ITRI offers Taiwan-domestic testing (L14-053); vendor in-house labs are the default substitute. Win by being the neutral, standards-cited reference with cheaper scripted benches.
timing_window_risk: Foreign-founder access moderate: test equipment is low-sensitivity; local certification politics may favor domestic labs - mitigate by selling benches to those labs rather than competing with them. Risk that CIEP/operators internalize testing fully.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-12-31: kill if no Chinese lab/vendor purchases a bench and no standards-body citation of the test methods by end-2034.
extreme_edge: Transient-calorimetry IP extends to qualifying MW-class EV charging cables and power-module cooling.
first_experiment: 2027-Q1: publish an instrumented round-robin of three commercial cold plates against their datasheet claims using a prototype 50 kW bench; success = reproducible discrepancy findings adopted/cited by at least one Chinese vendor or lab.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Import T/CIEP clause-scripted reporting and sell-to-domestic-certification-labs channel into C-05.

concept: 800 VDC rack-inlet power module for Chinese HVDC datacenters: hot-swap, pre-charge/inrush damping, bus-oscillation suppression, grounding/insulation monitoring in one qualified brick
named_buyer_examples: Delta Electronics (China)/Zhongheng-class rack power vendors (Panama HVDC lineage, L02-048)
Shenzhen Autrans - disclosed power supplier to Alibaba datacenters and Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan parks (L02-049)
Innoscience-ecosystem 800V rack integrators (L02-050)
painful_job: Chinese trade engineering coverage of 800V HVDC racks explicitly flags DC protection, grounding, hot-plug and inlet-node voltage oscillation as unresolved system-integration problems (L02-054); China's HVDC datacenter penetration lagged flagship deployments for years (~12% vs 78% UPS as of ~2021 context, L02-048), so the 2027+ 800V wave (NVIDIA-spec architecture, L02-043/044) hits integrators without a standard inlet-protection building block; DC-link capacitors remain a leading failure mechanism under higher density (L02-010).
product: A 30-100 kW 800V inlet module: solid-state pre-charge and inrush limiter, active damping of bus oscillation (mechanism: synthesized virtual impedance in a GaN/SiC series stage), hot-swap sequencing, insulation-monitoring/ground-fault location, and capacitor-health telemetry (ripple/ESR sensing per L02-010 failure physics). Drop-in ahead of existing PSU shelves (L02-046/051 device ecosystem).
frontier_vision: Own the 'DC utility interface' brick for every 800-1500 VDC rack and sidecar worldwide, expanding into vehicle megawatt charging inlets and industrial DC microgrid nodes.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: publish stability/oscillation models for 800V rack inlets; lab prototype of virtual-impedance damper at 10 kW; patents on combined hot-swap + damping + IMD architecture. 2027-2028: interop testing against OCP-style profiles in a university lab; engage two Chinese PSU vendors via joint test reports (pre-company technical collaboration). 2028-2029: 50 kW pilot brick validated with one integrator lab in Shenzhen/HK; reliability data package (capacitor telemetry validated against accelerated aging).
launch_2030_timing_thesis: 800VDC production racks scale from 2027 (L02-043/044); Chinese adoption historically lags flagship deployments by 2+ years (L02-048), putting the volume retrofit/greenfield wave at 2030-2033 - after the architecture is fixed but before integration niches are standardized.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: Alibaba-class operators extending HVDC (Panama lineage, L02-048/049) across Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan hub parks in the 15th FYP window, plus 800V rack refresh cycles tracking NVIDIA/OCP-spec architectures (L02-043, L02-044) as they localize in China (only mainland chip supplier Innoscience still 'in testing, no substantive orders' in 2025, L02-050 - i.e., the Chinese 800V supply base is still forming).
competition_outlook_2030: Huawei and Delta in-house teams solve this internally for their own racks (L02-054, L02-048); Autrans-class vendors add features piecemeal (L02-049); expect Innoscience-partner reference designs (L02-050). The niche survives if sold as a qualified module to second-tier integrators and colo operators who lack Huawei-scale engineering.
timing_window_risk: High domestic-substitution speed in Chinese power electronics; foreign founder must design-in via ODM/PSU partners (HK entity, licensing), not direct sales. Also NVIDIA-architecture localization uncertainty in China (export controls on GPUs) could fork the Chinese 800V spec - monitor.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if no Chinese integrator design-win (paid eval or license) by end-2033, since by then in-house solutions will have hardened.
extreme_edge: Virtual-impedance damping stage reused for 1500 VDC sidecars and MW EV-charging inlets.
first_experiment: 2027-Q3: demonstrate active suppression of inlet-bus oscillation and arc-free hot-swap at 800 V/10 kW on a hardware testbed replicating documented instability modes; success = stable insertion/removal under full load with <5% voltage overshoot.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Standalone CN-first dominated by Huawei/Delta in-house. Import virtual-impedance damping + capacitor telemetry into C-01.

concept: Modular solid-state DC protection stack (SSCB + arc-fault/traveling-wave detection) for Chinese datacenter parks and industrial LVDC/MVDC distribution
named_buyer_examples: Chinese datacenter power integrators (Delta China lineage L02-048; Autrans L02-049)
Industrial DC-yard EPCs serving electrolyzer/rectifier plants (tender ecosystem L11-051)
State-Grid-adjacent equipment channels as later expansion (XJ Electric tender scale, L08-034)
painful_job: DC faults have no natural zero-crossing; hybrid/solid-state breaker topologies remain non-converged active research through 2023-2025 (L08-001, L08-003, L08-004, L08-017), and DC arc-fault detection is still an open research problem even at low voltage (L08-019/020/021) - yet Chinese DC deployment is scaling on both ends: 800V datacenter buses with documented unresolved protection pain (L02-054) and RMB-billion-scale State Grid DC control/protection procurement (L08-034).
product: A scalable protection family: (1) 800-1500 V, 100 A-2 kA solid-state breaker modules (SiC + energy-absorbing branch, microsecond interruption); (2) arc-fault unit fusing high-frequency signature + traveling-wave detection (physics-based, per L08-019/020/021 methods) with selective coordination; (3) park-level protection coordination controller. Sold as bricks to integrators, not as turnkey switchgear.
frontier_vision: Become the 'ABB of DC' protection-component layer for the DC-electrified industrial world - datacenters, electrolyzer yards, mining microgrids, then meshed MVDC.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: comparative topology study + 800 V/500 A SSCB prototype in lab; publish interruption test data; patents on hybrid detection fusion. 2027-2028: build a DC fault-injection testbed; validate selectivity across a two-level DC network; engage Chinese integrator labs for joint fault-signature data collection. 2028-2029: 1500 V/1 kA pre-production unit + third-party witnessed short-circuit tests; customer discovery across 10+ Chinese DC integrators/EPCs.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Protection lags architecture: China's DC buildout (datacenter + industrial) is committing capital now while breaker technology is documented as unsettled; by 2030 the installed DC base needing retrofittable protection is large, and no dominant standard product will have emerged given 2018-2025 non-convergence.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) 800VDC datacenter park rollouts in Dong-Shu-Xi-Suan hubs (L02-049, L02-054); (b) State Grid's recurring UHV/DC control-and-protection tender rounds continuing into the 15th FYP (first-batch 2026 scale documented: XJ Electric RMB1.275bn, L08-034) pulling the domestic DC-protection supply chain upmarket; (c) GE Vernova-scale global datacenter electrification orders as corroborating macro (L08-033).
competition_outlook_2030: XJ Electric and State-Grid-affiliated protection houses dominate grid-side (L08-034); Huawei/Delta integrate rack-side in-house (L02-054, L02-048); academic SSCB IP will have spawned Chinese startups. Niche: park/industrial mid-layer where neither grid giants nor rack vendors focus, plus superior arc-fault physics.
timing_window_risk: Foreign-founder access hard at grid layer (skip it until 2032+); enter via industrial integrators and HK/bonded module sales. Certification (CCC/GB standards for DC switchgear) requires local partner - plan JV lab early.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no witnessed-certification unit and no integrator PO by mid-2034.
extreme_edge: Microsecond SSCB modules become enabling hardware for meshed MVDC grids - the documented long-term 'showstopper' niche.
first_experiment: 2027-Q4: interrupt a 800 V/500 A DC fault in <100 microseconds with a SiC SSCB prototype while an adjacent branch rides through (selectivity demo); success = repeatable interruption + zero nuisance trips across 1,000 fault/no-fault cycles.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Blend of C-01 and A-02; CCC/GB certification + JV requirements erase foreign-entrant margin by 2030.

concept: Tailored-voltage-waveform RF bias generator + fast multifrequency matching subsystem for Chinese etch/deposition tool OEMs (atomic-scale ion-energy control as a drop-in RF delivery upgrade)
named_buyer_examples: AMEC (etch revenue RMB7.28bn +54.7% in 2024, L06-048)
NAURA/Piotech/ACM Shanghai (documented subsystem buyers, L06-042/043; Q1-2026 growth L01-044)
end-pull from SMIC/YMTC fabs named in Hengyunchang IPO coverage (L06-043)
painful_job: China's RF-power components were reportedly <12% localized as of 2024 (L06-054) even as tool-level localization ran 21-35% (L06-051/052, contradictory methodologies preserved); the sole scaled domestic RF suppliers are fragile and customer-concentrated (Hengyunchang: one customer 45-63% of revenue, L06-042/043; Injet L06-044). Meanwhile ion-energy-distribution control via tailored voltage waveforms is a live 2024-2026 research frontier (L06-009/010/011/027; foundational matching demo L01-012) that no commodity generator ships as standard - Chinese OEMs need it to compete at advanced memory/logic etch.
product: An RF delivery subsystem: arbitrary-waveform bias supply (harmonic stacking to shape sheath voltage, hence ion energy distribution) + frequency-agile solid-state match achieving sub-ms retuning, with pulse-synchronous metrology. Mechanism: multi-harmonic synthesis with real-time sheath-model feedback replaces fixed-frequency sine bias, enabling narrow IED control for ALE/HAR etch.
frontier_vision: Displace the AE/MKS duopoly's next-generation sockets across Asia with waveform-defined plasma power, extending to deposition uniformity control and industrial plasma chemistry.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: build waveform-synthesis prototype on a lab ICP/CCP reactor; replicate published IED-control results (L06-009-011); file patents outside blocked jurisdictions carefully (export-control counsel first - see risk). 2027-2028: joint experiments with a university plasma lab; develop match-speed benchmark data vs commercial units (L01-049/050 spec envelope). 2028-2029: demo on a Chinese OEM's development chamber via joint-research agreement; qualify BOM for non-US-origin supply where lawful.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: AE's own next-gen platforms were only 'early pilot/production' in Feb 2026 (L06-039), i.e., the waveform-control generation of RF delivery is still being productized industry-wide; Chinese OEMs' localization pressure peaks in the 15th FYP - a 2030 entrant hits the second wave of domestic RF sockets before they commoditize.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: supplier-localization programs of AMEC/NAURA/Piotech under the 15th FYP semiconductor self-sufficiency push, evidenced by the Hengyunchang/Injet domestic-substitution wave (IPO Jan-2026, L06-042/043/044) and the <12% component-localization gap (L06-054) that those OEMs are documented to be closing.
competition_outlook_2030: Hengyunchang and Injet will have matured (L06-042/044); AE/MKS/Comet will ship waveform features (L06-039, L01-049/050); expect CAS-spinoff entrants. Win only with demonstrably better IED control + retune speed and OEM co-development lock-in.
timing_window_risk: SEVERE export-control/access risk: advanced-node etch subsystems sold to SMIC/YMTC-linked OEMs may be restricted for US persons/technology; structure around non-US IP, target legacy/compound-semi/panel-level tools first, obtain counsel before any China engagement. Also Chinese OEMs may vertically integrate RF (Piotech owns a stake in Hengyunchang, L06-042).
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if export-control analysis blocks the lawful channel or no OEM joint-development agreement by end-2033.
extreme_edge: Waveform-defined power scales down to atomic-layer processing and up to industrial plasma chemistry reactors.
first_experiment: 2027-Q2: on a lab CCP reactor, demonstrate a synthesized 3-harmonic bias waveform narrowing measured ion-energy distribution width >=2x vs single-frequency bias at equal power; budget covers reactor time, RF chain, and retarding-field analyzer.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Self-declared severe export-control risk on the core channel is the thesis, not a footnote; CN opportunity survives only as A-10's partitioned >=28nm line.

concept: Electrostatic-chuck power/control subsystem: HV clamp supply with Johnsen-Rahbek leakage compensation, fast declamp, and multi-zone temperature-uniformity electronics for Chinese tool OEMs
named_buyer_examples: AMEC/NAURA/Piotech etch-deposition OEMs (L06-048, L06-042/043)
Chinese ESC/ceramics houses needing the electronics half (component-localization gap context, L06-054)
painful_job: ESC behavior is an unsolved coupled problem - dielectric breakdown limits (L06-005), Johnsen-Rahbek charging/discharge dynamics under plasma (L06-006), AlN resistivity tuning (L06-014), and thermal-uniformity control (L06-007, L06-015) - and a Chinese design-space framework paper (L06-004) shows domestic developers are attacking it now; but Chinese OEMs still import the qualified clamp-power/control stack, part of the <12% RF/precision-component localization gap (L06-054).
product: A qualified ESC electronics module: +/-kV programmable clamp supply with real-time leakage-current-based clamp-force estimation (mechanism: JR contact-current model inversion), arc-safe fast declamp with residual-charge neutralization sequencing, and 10+ zone heater drive with mK-class uniformity control - sold to OEMs and Chinese ceramics makers as the electronic half of a domestic ESC stack. Initial scope (FIX): mature-node (>=28nm) and panel/display tools only, for export-control safety; leading-edge sockets deferred until counsel clears.
frontier_vision: Standard electronics layer for every electrostatic clamping application - semicon, panel, e-beam tools, space robotics grippers.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: lab testbed replicating JR clamp physics on commercial ceramic pucks; publish clamp-force estimation validation; patents on leakage-based force observer + declamp sequencing. 2027-2028: co-development with a university plasma lab to characterize declamp-induced wafer displacement; build reliability data. 2028-2029: joint-evaluation with one Chinese ceramics maker + one OEM dev chamber; pre-qualify supply chain.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Chinese ESC localization is behind whole-tool localization; ceramics capability is emerging domestically (L06-004 research base) but the control-electronics half has no focused domestic supplier - 2030 lands after ceramics mature and before electronics commoditize.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: same 15th-FYP OEM supplier-localization wave (Hengyunchang/Injet precedent L06-042/044; component gap L06-054) extended to ESC subsystems as AMEC/NAURA move to higher-aspect-ratio etch where clamp/thermal control binds (etch growth documented L06-048). FIX condition: this trigger is inferential (localization gap + research base), not a named ESC tender; P4 must produce a named ESC-localization program/tender or a ceramics-maker co-development LOI before this seed can be promoted.
competition_outlook_2030: Incumbent ESC vendors (US/JP) ship integrated solutions; Chinese ceramics entrants will seek electronics partners rather than build - the seed's channel; risk of AE/MKS bundling clamp supplies (L06-039). P4 verification task: confirm AE/MKS clamp-supply bundling (L06-039) does not foreclose the merchant socket.
timing_window_risk: Same export-control caution as B-05 (advanced-node exposure) though ESC electronics are less node-specific; foreign founder should target mature-node and panel/display tools first. Flag: ESC-specific China demand evidence is inferential (L06-004 + localization gap), not a named tender - verify in P4.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if no ceramics-maker or OEM co-development agreement by end-2033.
extreme_edge: Clamp-force observer physics reused for electrostatic handling in vacuum robotics and lithography reticle chucks.
first_experiment: 2027-Q3: demonstrate real-time clamp-force estimation within +/-10% of load-cell ground truth across temperature and plasma-exposure aging on a JR ceramic puck; success includes <100 ms residual-charge-safe declamp.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: product scoped to mature-node/panel tools; demand explicitly labeled inferential pending a named ESC-localization program/tender or ceramics-maker LOI (P4 gate); AE/MKS bundling-foreclosure check added.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Genuinely under-picked subsystem; demand currently inferential (self-flagged).

concept: Thermal-shock-tolerant printed-circuit heat exchanger (PCHE) recuperator product line for China's sCO2 waste-heat-recovery retrofit wave
named_buyer_examples: CNNC/Nuclear Power Institute of China - operator of the 30 MW commercial 'Chaotan One' sCO2 unit (L04-048, L04-051)
CAS Institute of Engineering Thermophysics sCO2 programs (L04-029, L04-106, L04-109)
Shanghai Marine Diesel Engine Institute compressor program (L04-102)
steel-plant WHR retrofit EPCs (archetype per L04-051 retrofit framing)
painful_job: PCHE recuperators are the most-cited technical bottleneck in sCO2 systems, and CAS-IET's 2025 work explicitly targets rapid-thermal-cycling tolerance (up to 22C/min) as an unsolved problem (L04-029); China's retrofit fleet (steel sintering WHR) needs recuperators that survive industrial load transients, and the only quantified market anchor is a single-source ~RMB100bn/~300-unit state-media estimate (L04-051 - flagged, needs P4 triangulation), with no commercially sourceable rapid-cycle-rated PCHE line outside national-lab prototypes.
product: A PCHE recuperator family engineered for thermal shock: graded-channel diffusion-bonded cores with compliant headers (mechanism: distributing transient thermal strain away from bond planes), creep-qualified alloys per established test methods (materials challenges catalog, L04-113), delivered with cycling-rated warranties and instrumented health monitoring.
frontier_vision: Become the qualified compact-heat-exchanger vendor for the global sCO2/advanced-cycle era - waste heat, CSP+storage, nuclear BOP - the 'Heatric of the transient age'.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: university-hosted design + coupon-level diffusion-bonding trials; thermal-shock rig testing bond integrity at >20C/min cycles; 2 patents (graded channels, compliant header). 2027-2028: sub-scale (100 kW-class) core validated against cycling life model; publish. 2028-2029: joint test with a Chinese institute program (CAS-IET/SMDERI channel) under research agreement; line-design for pilot manufacturing with a HK/JV structure.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: China is the only country with a commercial sCO2 WHR unit operating (Nov-Dec 2025, L04-048/051); follow-on units through 2028 (Xinjiang project) create a qualified-vendor vacuum for cycling-rated recuperators exactly when a 2030 company can supply - too early for commoditization, physics already proven.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: CNNC's Xinjiang molten-salt + sCO2 demonstration (construction H1-2026, completion target 2028) carries the national 'first-set major technical equipment' designation that typically unlocks follow-on procurement (L04-051); the steel-sintering retrofit pipeline (Chaotan One replication, L04-048/051) is the 2030-2034 volume driver.
competition_outlook_2030: CAS-IET/CNNC in-house fabrication (L04-029, L04-048) is the main rival - the play is to be their qualified merchant supplier; global PCHE incumbents lack China channels and cycling-rated products; expect 1-2 Chinese HX makers entering via national programs.
timing_window_risk: Foreign founder selling into CNNC-adjacent projects requires JV/localization; IP protection in diffusion-bonding process know-how is trade-secret-heavy (defensible). Market size rests on one state-media figure (L04-051) - must be re-verified before scale investment.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no Chinese integrator purchase order or JV manufacturing agreement by mid-2034.
extreme_edge: Same cores serve fusion blanket/divertor-adjacent heat exchange and hypersonic thermal management.
first_experiment: 2027-Q4: coupon-scale graded-channel PCHE core survives 1,000 thermal-shock cycles at >=20C/min between 200C and 550C with <5% effectiveness degradation and zero bond-line cracks (dye-penetrant + CT verified).
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Import graded-channel/compliant-header mechanism and CNNC first-set logic; RMB100bn TAM stays single-source-flagged.

concept: sCO2 turbomachinery sealing and bearing modules: engineered dry-gas/elastohydrodynamic shaft seals and gas-foil bearings for Chinese sCO2 compressor/turbine programs
named_buyer_examples: CNNC/NPIC sCO2 units (L04-048, L04-051)
Shanghai Marine Diesel Engine Institute 100-150 kW compressor program (L04-102)
CAS-IET MWe compressor and turbine cooling programs (L04-106, L04-109)
painful_job: Seals and bearings are named, catalogued sCO2 challenges (Sandia's five materials/turbomachinery challenges include gas-foil bearings and degradation, L04-113); a 2024-2025 elastohydrodynamic sCO2 seal is still a novel research artifact (L04-101); China runs at least three parallel compressor programs (L04-102/106) plus commercial units (L04-048/051) - all needing seals/bearings that survive high-density CO2 windage, with no merchant qualified supplier in the atlas.
product: A qualified module set: (1) film-riding dry-gas seals tuned for dense-CO2 (mechanism: groove geometry + EHD compliance handling phase-change margins per L04-101 physics); (2) gas-foil bearing cartridges with engineered coatings for 27,000+ rpm class machines; (3) instrumented seal-health telemetry. Sold as drop-in cartridges to institute-led OEM programs.
frontier_vision: The SKF-of-sCO2: sealing/bearing cartridges for every dense-fluid machine - sCO2, ORC, heat pumps, hydrogen turbomachinery.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: seal test rig (static + dynamic) in university lab; replicate EHD seal results (L04-101) at representative pressure; foil-bearing coating trials. 2027-2028: 30,000 rpm spin-rig validation; publish leakage/life data; 2 patents. 2028-2029: joint validation on a Chinese institute loop via research collaboration; pre-negotiate JV supply structure.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: China's sCO2 machines move from demo to fleet in 2028-2032 (Xinjiang completion 2028, L04-051); seal/bearing wear becomes the fleet's availability constraint precisely then; global suppliers have no cycling-rated dense-CO2 cartridge line.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: follow-on units after CNNC's 'first-set' Xinjiang designation (L04-051) and Chaotan One replication across sintering WHR (L04-048); institute compressor programs (L04-102/106) become OEMs needing merchant components.
competition_outlook_2030: Institute in-house solutions and legacy seal vendors adapting oil-and-gas dry-gas seals; differentiation = dense-CO2-specific physics + telemetry. Narrow niche - acceptable only as beachhead into broader dense-fluid machinery.
timing_window_risk: Very narrow initial SAM; foreign-founder access via joint development only; institute procurement may be China-only (JV required). Rate as extra/optional seed.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if no institute co-test agreement by end-2033.
extreme_edge: Dense-fluid film-riding seal physics extends to supercritical hydrogen and cryogenic turbopumps.
first_experiment: 2028-Q1: dynamic seal rig demonstrates <0.5% leakage at representative dense-CO2 conditions over 500 h with no face damage; foil bearing survives 100 start-stop cycles at 30 krpm.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Self-labeled optional; narrow SAM, JV-only access. C-19 carries the concept.

concept: Standardized solid-state pulsed-power modulator platform (50-150 kV IGBT Marx family) for China's industrial e-beam, sterilization, NDT and medical accelerator buildout
named_buyer_examples: CGN Dasheng Accelerator Technology - RMB340M newly signed H1-2025 contracts, +21% YoY (L05-035)
China Isotope & Radiation Corp / Atom Hi-Tech - 2024 centralized self-shielded cyclotron tender for three medical-isotope centers (L05-036)
IHEP/CEPC RF program as high-end expansion channel (L05-012, L05-013)
painful_job: Every accelerator project integrates a bespoke modulator: at least five independent, non-interoperable solid-state modulator/SSPA designs documented (L05-003 through L05-009), no accelerator-specific pulsed-power acceptance standard beyond generic IEC HV tests, and vacuum-device output couplers remain a top failure item at high CW power (L05-014). China's industrial-accelerator demand is growing and disclosed (L05-035, L05-036) while modulator supply is fragmented imports (ScandiNova L05-047, Jema L05-030) or in-house builds.
product: A modular IGBT-Marx modulator platform: 50-150 kV, 100 A-class, ms-pulse family built from one qualified 3 kV brick (mechanism: series-stacked bricks with per-stage droop correction and fiber-synchronized gate timing), standard control/diagnostic API, klystron/magnetron/e-beam-gun variants - sold to Chinese accelerator integrators and system OEMs.
frontier_vision: The 'server-power-supply moment' for pulsed power: one standardized brick family powering sterilization, NDT, cargo scanning, medical linacs, and eventually collider RF stations.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: design + single-brick (3 kV) qualification; assemble 10-stage 30 kV stack in university lab; publish droop-correction performance. 2027-2028: 100 kV/50 A prototype driving a dummy klystron load; reliability run (10^7 pulses); patents on brick interconnect + fault-isolation. 2028-2029: co-test with an Asian accelerator lab; customer discovery with Chinese integrators (CGN Dasheng channel via HK trading structures where lawful); pre-certify to IEC HV test methods.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: China's e-beam/isotope infrastructure is scaling on disclosed multi-hundred-MRMB order books now (L05-035/036); modulators are the recurring cost/reliability bottleneck; no standard platform exists globally (documented fragmentation), so a 2030 productized brick family enters an unconsolidated niche that big vendors ignore (too custom) and labs under-productize.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) CGN Dasheng's accelerator order-book growth trajectory (L05-035) implying fleet buildout and replacement demand into 2030-2034; (b) China Isotope & Radiation's medical-isotope center program (L05-036) requiring cyclotron/linac power systems; (c) CEPC decision window - IHEP's high-efficiency klystron program (78.5% achieved, 80.5% target, L05-012/013) creates a named future demand pole for standardized modulators if approved in the 15th FYP (approval uncertainty flagged).
competition_outlook_2030: ScandiNova (L05-047) and Jema (L05-030) defend the import channel; CGN/CAEP in-house teams build for themselves; expect a Chinese modulator startup from the CAS ecosystem. Win via cost-per-pulse reliability data and the standardized-brick logistics story.
timing_window_risk: Nuclear-SOE procurement (CGN) is hard for foreign vendors - enter via medical/NDT integrators and JV licensing; dual-use export-control review required for high-power pulsed hardware. Access risk medium-high.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no Chinese integrator purchase/license and no lab co-test by mid-2034.
extreme_edge: Same bricks scale to fusion-adjacent LTD-style drivers and DEW pulse supplies (export-controlled paths excluded from plan).
first_experiment: 2027-Q3: 10-stage Marx stack delivers 30 kV/50 A, 1 ms flat-top pulses with <1% droop at 100 Hz for 10^6 pulses without stage failure; success also requires demonstrated single-stage fault ride-through.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Nuclear-SOE access foreign-hostile. Import 3kV brick + droop-correction detail and CIRC tender evidence into C-09 CN chapter.

concept: HTS conductor and coil QC instrumentation: reel-to-reel critical-current/defect mapping plus quench-detection/protection electronics for Chinese tape makers and fusion magnet programs
named_buyer_examples: Shanghai Superconductor / Shanghai International Superconducting Tech (built and operates the State Grid Shanghai 35 kV commercial HTS cable, L03-041, L03-052)
Western Superconducting Technologies (ITER strand supplier, L03-031)
ASIPP Hefei (35.1 T hybrid magnet record 2025, L03-029/L03-037; CFETR magnet engineering L03-024)
painful_job: REBCO conductors degrade via strain, delamination and screening-current stress at high field (L03-018, L03-020, L03-021); tape capacity is concentrated in under 10 vendors and single fusion orders can absorb large shares of output (L03-019), so every meter shipped to a magnet program must be QC-mapped and every coil needs trustworthy quench detection - while Chinese buyers (State Grid cable projects L03-041, ASIPP magnets L03-029) are scaling faster than any standardized QC/quench instrumentation supply.
product: Two coupled product lines: (1) reel-to-reel Ic + defect mapper (mechanism: continuous transport-current/contactless Hall-array scanning with in-line strain stress screening) producing per-meter conductor passports; (2) coil quench-detection/protection electronics using multi-modal sensing (voltage taps + fast fiber/acoustic channels) with microsecond discrimination logic, sold as instrument racks to tape factories, cable projects, and magnet labs.
frontier_vision: Become the metrology-and-protection layer of the global HTS industry - every tape meter passported, every magnet protected - expanding into cable commissioning and in-service monitoring.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: build benchtop Ic scanner (LN2, 77 K) in university lab; validate against tape samples from multiple vendors; publish defect-detection sensitivity study. 2027-2028: quench-detection testbed on small REBCO coils; multi-modal discrimination algorithms validated against induced quenches; patents. 2028-2029: pilot passporting service with one tape maker (HK/Shanghai research collaboration), spec productized reel-to-reel line; engage fusion-program QA teams on requirements.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: HTS magnet demand inflects with fusion programs and grid cables in the late 2020s; QC/protection instrumentation is the picks-and-shovels layer that scales with conductor volume regardless of which magnet projects win; nobody has standardized it (bespoke acceptance testing documented), and China's conductor base is expanding to reduce import dependence (L03-052).
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) CFETR/China fusion magnet procurement decision expected post-ITER in the 15th FYP window (design complete, construction start undated - uncertainty explicitly flagged, L03-024); (b) ASIPP's escalating high-field program (35.1 T record 2025, L03-029/037); (c) State Grid HTS cable follow-ons after the Shanghai 'fully commercial' installation (L03-041 - single-installation caveat preserved).
competition_outlook_2030: Tape makers' in-house rigs (default substitute); instrument boutiques in EU/JP; Chinese institutes building their own. Win via throughput (reel-to-reel speed), passport data infrastructure, and neutrality between vendor and buyer.
timing_window_risk: Foreign-founder access comparatively good (instrumentation, not controlled magnet tech), but fusion-program procurement may be China-only - sell to tape merchants and via HK entity; CFETR timing slip is the main demand risk (explicitly undated in atlas).
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-12-31: kill if neither a tape-maker install nor a fusion-program order by end-2034.
extreme_edge: Quench-discrimination electronics evolve into flight-grade protection for HTS motors/generators.
first_experiment: 2027-Q2: benchtop reel-to-reel scanner detects seeded micro-defects (>=90% detection, <5% false positive) at >=10 m/min on commercial REBCO tape at 77 K; success benchmarked against destructive Ic sampling.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Bundles two canonicals (tape QC->D-02, quench->D-01). Import conductor-passport framing and CN buyer set into D-02.

concept: Skid-mounted kW-class 4-20 K cryogenic plant modules for mid-scale Chinese HTS, accelerator, and fusion-adjacent programs (between lab cryocoolers and bespoke helium plants)
named_buyer_examples: ASIPP magnet facilities (L03-029/037)
State Grid HTS cable cooling stations (Shanghai cable operating since 2021, L03-041)
IHEP-class facility programs (procurement style evidenced by HEPS tenders, L07-045)
painful_job: Catalog cryocoolers deliver mW-to-W class cooling (PT205: 10 mW at 2.5 K for 1.1 kW input, L03-050) while flagship plants are bespoke kW-class installations (JT-60SA ~9.5 kW-equivalent context, L03-025/026); mid-scale magnet/cable projects have no standardized, repeatable 100 W-1 kW @ 4-20 K product, creating cost/schedule risk for every new Chinese HTS deployment (cable stations L03-041; high-field labs L03-029).
product: Standardized cryoplant skids: turbo-Brayton/pulse-tube hybrid modules delivering 100 W-1 kW at 10-20 K (HTS regime) with N+1 redundancy, automated cooldown control, and remote monitoring - productized like industrial chillers rather than engineered plants.
frontier_vision: Cryogenics-as-a-utility for the superconducting economy: fleets of standard skids cooling cables, fault-current limiters, magnets and quantum facilities.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: thermodynamic architecture study + compressor/expander vendor mapping; sub-scale (50 W @ 20 K) lab demonstrator. 2027-2028: reliability engineering of the skid controller; publish cost-per-watt analysis vs bespoke plants; patents on cooldown-automation and redundancy switchover. 2028-2029: partner-lab field trial adjacent to an HTS testbed; JV/licensing discussions with Chinese cryo manufacturers.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: HTS deployments multiply post-2030 (fusion + grid); the mid-scale cooling gap is documented and persistent; incumbents build projects, not products - a productized skid line at 2030 rides the deployment curve.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: CFETR/BEST-era Chinese fusion facility buildout (post-ITER decision window, L03-024 - undated, flagged) and State Grid HTS cable replication beyond Shanghai (L03-041); ASIPP facility expansion (L03-029/037).
competition_outlook_2030: Global cryoplant majors (bespoke, expensive), Chinese cryocooler makers scaling up, institute in-house plants. Differentiation: standardization + fleet reliability. Note: competitive intel in atlas is thin (vendor spec L03-050 only) - P4 must map Chinese cryo industry before promotion.
timing_window_risk: Capital-intensive; China-only procurement rules at big-science buyers (L07-045) force JV; Chinese cryo industry may close this gap domestically before 2030 - treat as optional/extra seed pending P4 competitor mapping.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-12-31: kill if P4 competitor mapping shows domestic skid products already shipping, or no partner-lab trial by end-2033.
extreme_edge: Same skids serve hydrogen-liquefaction boil-off management and space-simulation chambers.
first_experiment: 2028-Q2: 50 W @ 20 K demonstrator achieves >=95% availability over a 1,000 h unattended run with automated cooldown/recovery; cost model validated within 20%.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Atlas competitive intel on Chinese cryo industry admittedly absent; mapping task moves to C-12 FIX.

concept: NEG-coating and low-outgassing surface-engineering line (equipment + licensed process) for Chinese UHV chamber fabricators serving accelerators, quantum labs, and semicon
named_buyer_examples: China-registered vacuum-chamber manufacturers who alone may bid IHEP/HEPS-class tenders (China-only bid rule documented, L07-045)
IHEP itself for beampipe processing capacity (L07-045)
Chinese quantum/semicon chamber fabricators (UHV demand fronts, L07-030-class context)
painful_job: Hydrogen outgassing control requires energy- and schedule-intensive bakeout (250C/30 h for >70,000x reduction, L07-009; supporting physics L07-008), and distributed NEG pumping - academically mature since the 1990s (L07-001) - is commercially concentrated in essentially one vendor (SAES, L07-037), while China's big-science vacuum procurement is restricted to domestic manufacturers (L07-045) who lack an in-country NEG-coating/outgassing-engineering supply base.
product: A licensed production cell: magnetron-sputtered TiZrV NEG coating line for beampipes/chambers (mechanism: getter film pumps residual gas after low-temperature activation, replacing lumped pumps), plus vacuum-firing/bakeout optimization service and witness-sample outgassing metrology - sold as equipment + process license + QA protocol to Chinese fabricators.
frontier_vision: The surface-engineering layer of the vacuum world: NEG-coated compact systems for accelerators, quantum hardware, and eventually portable UHV instruments.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: replicate TiZrV coating performance on a lab sputtering rig; build outgassing metrology bench (throughput method); publish activation-temperature/pumping-speed data. 2027-2028: pilot-coat real beampipe geometries; develop QA witness protocol; patents on coating-uniformity fixtures for long narrow pipes. 2028-2029: license negotiation with 1-2 Chinese chamber makers (structure: HK IP entity + domestic licensee to fit the China-only tender rule); requirements discovery with IHEP vacuum group.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: China's light-source/collider pipeline keeps procuring domestically (HEPS precedent, L07-045); the NEG single-vendor global bottleneck (L07-037) plus domestic-bid rules make a licensed in-China coating cell the natural 2030 entry - low commoditization risk because process know-how, not hardware, is the moat.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: post-HEPS Chinese synchrotron/FEL construction and - if approved in the 15th FYP - CEPC vacuum-system procurement (IHEP program evidenced via its klystron/RF investments, L05-012/013; approval uncertainty flagged); each such machine needs kilometers of NEG-coated chamber from China-registered suppliers (L07-045).
competition_outlook_2030: SAES defending via export sales (L07-037); IHEP in-house coating labs; possibly a CAS spinoff. The licensed-cell model co-opts rather than fights domestic fabricators.
timing_window_risk: Demand is lumpy and government-approval-dependent (CEPC undecided); foreign founder cannot bid directly - licensing structure mandatory; IP leakage risk in process licensing is real (mitigate via consumables + QA lock-ins).
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-12-31: kill if no Chinese license agreement and no new large CN accelerator project approved by end-2034.
extreme_edge: NEG-coated compact chambers enable maintenance-free UHV for portable quantum sensors.
first_experiment: 2027-Q3: lab-coated 2 m narrow-bore pipe demonstrates specified pumping speed and >=20 activation cycles without performance loss, verified on an in-house outgassing bench against published TiZrV benchmarks.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): License-into-China model = IP leakage in a process-know-how business; CEPC-dependent lumpy demand; licensee accumulates the asset.

concept: Low-ripple high-current rectification and power-quality retrofit systems (IGBT/MMC hybrid + electrochemical-aware control) for Chinese green-hydrogen plants and electrowinning tank houses
named_buyer_examples: Sinopec (Kuqa 260 MW project documented running below nameplate, L11-049)
China Energy Construction (125-set alkaline+PEM tender, L11-051)
LONGi Hydrogen / Peric / Sungrow Hydrogen as OEM/EPC channels (L11-050, L11-051)
expansion: Chinese copper electrowinning tank houses (rectifier-quality lever, L11-045; electro-refining literature L11-026/027)
painful_job: Six-pulse thyristor rectifiers remain the MW-scale default; documented penalty: up to ~14% specific-energy and ~13% loss reduction available from transistor/MMC-class supplies (L11-016 through L11-021), and ripple/intermittency measurably accelerates stack degradation under renewable coupling (L11-001, L11-003, L11-004, L11-031); Sinopec's flagship Kuqa ran at a fraction of nameplate (L11-049) - power delivery and variable-operation control, not stacks, are the binding quality problem in a market where Chinese stack overcapacity (~2x demand, L11-035, L11-051) has commoditized the cell itself.
product: A rectification/power-quality retrofit skid: IGBT active front end + interleaved DC stage with <1% current ripple at MW scale, electrochemical-aware control (mechanism: degradation-weighted current scheduling that maps renewable variability into stack-safe trajectories per documented stressor physics), harmonic compliance, and per-stack ripple analytics. Same hardware core serves electrolyzers and electrowinning lines (L11-045).
frontier_vision: Own the precision-DC layer of electrochemical industry worldwide - hydrogen, electrowinning, chlor-alkali, e-fuels - selling efficiency as retrofittable hardware.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: hardware-in-loop testbed correlating ripple spectra with degradation proxies on short stacks (replicating L11-016/021 findings); publish; patents on degradation-weighted scheduling. 2027-2028: 100 kW-class AFE+DC prototype with measured efficiency delta vs thyristor baseline; engage one Chinese EPC and one electrowinning operator on retrofit economics (site data collection under NDA-free academic framing). 2028-2029: containerized 1 MW pilot design; pre-agreement with an OEM/EPC channel (LONGi/Peric-class) for 2030 co-deployment.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Atlas negative finding respected: no stack play - Chinese overcapacity kills stack margins (L11-035/051) but strengthens the efficiency-differentiation case for balance-of-plant power quality; renewable-coupled megaprojects keep tendering (L11-049/050/051) and by 2030 the installed base of underperforming thyristor-fed plants is itself the retrofit market.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: continuing Sinopec / China Energy Construction electrolyzer tender cadence in Xinjiang/Inner Mongolia renewable-hydrogen bases documented through 2025-2026 (L11-049, L11-050, L11-051) extending into the 15th FYP; retrofit demand from the documented utilization shortfall at flagship sites (L11-049). 15th-FYP-specific hydrogen targets must be re-verified in P4 (2026 policy baseline only).
competition_outlook_2030: Sungrow - a power-electronics giant already inside hydrogen tenders (L11-051) - is the decisive competitive threat; also rectifier incumbents and OEM in-house BOP. Survive only via electrochemical-aware control IP + neutrality across stack OEMs; if Sungrow ships equivalent ripple specs by 2029, kill early.
timing_window_risk: High: Chinese power-electronics cost curve is brutal; SOE project entry requires EPC partnering; foreign founder must license or JV. Electrowinning expansion lacks named Chinese buyer evidence in atlas (L11-045 is vendor-side) - flagged for P4.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2033-06-30: kill if Sungrow/domestic equivalents reach <1% ripple MW-class products before we have a pilot PO (check annually), or no EPC partnership by mid-2033.
extreme_edge: Degradation-aware DC scheduling generalizes to battery formation and CO2 electrolysis when those mature.
first_experiment: 2027-Q2: on a short-stack testbed, demonstrate measured specific-energy improvement >=8% and a quantified degradation-rate reduction under emulated wind/solar profiles for IGBT low-ripple supply vs 6-pulse thyristor baseline over 1,000 h.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (REJECT): Sungrow as buyer-and-giant-competitor kills CN-primary standalone. Import Kuqa retrofit evidence + degradation-weighted scheduling into C-07.

concept: Dual-standard megawatt charging/battery-swap interface power conversion: one qualified converter family bridging Chinese GB/T swap ecosystems and IEC TS 63379 MCS static charging for exported mining/port fleets
named_buyer_examples: XCMG - 240t BEV truck supplier to Fortescue with phased 2028-2030 deliveries into an MCS/static-charging market (L10-028)
SPIC Qiyuan - battery-swap mining-truck technology exporter (Oyu Tolgoi deployment, L10-031)
Chinese swap-standard ecosystem players under the national excavator battery-swap standard (L10-046) and CCS battery-ship rules (L10-045)
painful_job: China bet on battery-swap across trucks, excavators and vessels (L10-031, L10-040, L10-045, L10-046) while Western mine/port buyers standardize on CharIN/IEC MCS static megawatt charging (IEC TS 63379, published Feb-2026, L10-050); the atlas documents no cross-recognition between the two ecosystems, so Chinese OEMs exporting equipment (XCMG-Fortescue, L10-028; Liebherr trolley-compatible trucks, L10-052) must engineer bespoke power interfaces per project - a recurring, standards-driven integration tax.
product: A qualified bidirectional 1-3.75 MW DC converter family with swappable interface personalities: GB/T swap-station DC bus profile on one side, IEC TS 63379 MCS (up to 1,250 V/3,000 A) on the other (mechanism: modular SiC DC-DC bricks + certified protocol/interlock stacks for each standard), enabling one truck/vessel platform to serve both ecosystems.
frontier_vision: The interoperability layer of global heavy-fleet electrification - every exported Chinese machine and every mixed-standard mine site runs through our conversion/interlock stack.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: standards deep-dive + protocol/interlock reference implementation for IEC TS 63379 and GB swap profiles; desk validation with CharIN documentation; patents on dual-personality safety interlock arbitration. 2027-2028: 250 kW brick prototype demonstrating both profiles on a battery emulator; publish interop test methodology. 2028-2029: field data partnership targeting one OEM export program (XCMG/SPIC channel via HK entity); pre-certification plan with a test lab. FIX gate (2028): documented evidence that one named export program (XCMG-Fortescue delivery chain or an SPIC swap-export follow-on) will procure dual-standard interface hardware; without it the standalone thesis ends.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: XCMG's Fortescue deliveries run 2028-2030 (L10-028) and swap-vs-MCS non-convergence is documented as of 2026; by 2030 the dual-ecosystem friction is an operating cost with named fleets on both sides - and no converged standard is plausible given three architectures still competing (L10-028/029/031 pattern).
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: (a) XCMG-Fortescue phased 240t BEV deliveries 2028-2030 and follow-on Western-market export bids (L10-028); (b) SPIC swap-technology export expansion after Oyu Tolgoi trial concludes end-2026 (L10-031); (c) domestic SOE open-pit equipment tender cadence (China Gold 60-truck-class tenders, L10-034; Baogang retrofit programs, L10-035) electrifying under swap standards (L10-046).
competition_outlook_2030: OEM in-house power teams (XCMG), CATL swap ecosystem (L10-031/040), Western charger vendors implementing MCS only. The neutral dual-standard niche survives if certification burden stays high - which IEC TS 63379's 2026 vintage suggests.
timing_window_risk: Foreign founder is actually advantaged on the IEC/Western-certification side; China-side access via OEM design-in. Risk: a swap-standard export failure (Western mines rejecting swap) would collapse the dual-standard need into MCS-only - monitor Fortescue/Rio architecture choices (L10-028/031). Fold-in path (pre-agreed per adjudication): if the swap architecture fails offshore, the dual-personality converter folds into P3R2-A-21 as its interface-personality module rather than continuing standalone. Demand classification: contingent (an option on standards non-convergence), not a base-demand seed.
commercial_readiness_kill_date: 2034-06-30: kill if no OEM co-development agreement by mid-2034 or if MCS-vs-swap converges to one survivor before 2032.
extreme_edge: Protocol-arbitrated MW interfaces extend to port cranes, e-vessel shore/swap hybrid stations (L10-040/045).
first_experiment: 2028-Q1: 250 kW dual-personality converter passes scripted conformance suites for both IEC TS 63379 signaling and a GB swap-station DC profile on a battery emulator, including cross-standard fault-interlock scenarios.
fix_applied_notes: 2026-07-13 FIX applied: 2028 named-export-program procurement-evidence gate added; fold-in path to A-21 pre-agreed; demand reclassified as contingent standards-arbitrage option, not base demand.
adjudication_note: 2026-07-13 adjudication (FIX): Genuine standards-arbitrage insight; contingent demand, not a base.

concept: Industrial plasma power-and-matching platform (pulsed DC/RF with arc management) for Chinese non-semiconductor plasma equipment: PECVD coating, PCB plasma cleaning, surface treatment
named_buyer_examples: Chinese PCB makers tendering plasma cleaning machines (Wuxi Shennan Circuit tender, L01-114)
defense-industrial PECVD buyers evidencing institutional demand (AVIC Xi'an FACRI award, L01-039 - demand signal, not a target account)
Chinese plasma-tool OEM ecosystem growing with NAURA/Piotech/ACM (L01-044)
painful_job: Chinese industrial (non-semi) plasma equipment builders buy imported RF generators/matches (AE/MKS/Comet/TRUMPF envelope: 13-60 MHz, 0.6-6 kW, pulsing to 100 kHz - L01-049/050, L01-040) at premium prices with slow support, while frontier multi-frequency matching remains research-grade (L01-012); PCB and coating tenders show steady mid-market demand (L01-114, L01-039) that neither the duopoly's premium lines nor bare-bones local supplies serve with modern pulsing/arc-management.
product: A mid-power (1-30 kW) plasma power platform: pulsed-DC and RF variants with integrated fast matching, arc detection/quench (sub-microsecond energy diversion), recipe-level digital control, and fleet telemetry - positioned between imported premium units and commodity domestic supplies for coating, cleaning, and treatment tools.
frontier_vision: The power-and-control brain of industrial plasma processing across Asia, later feeding chemistry-grade waveform control into plasma nitriding, ozone, and materials synthesis.
precompany_plan_2026_2029: 2026-2027: prototype pulsed-DC 5 kW unit with arc-quench bench validated on a lab magnetron/PECVD target; benchmark vs published incumbent specs (L01-049/050). 2027-2028: RF variant + matching module; reliability run; patents on arc-energy diversion topology. 2028-2029: field beta on one Chinese PCB/coating line via equipment-maker collaboration (HK entity); cost-down BOM for China manufacturing license.
launch_2030_timing_thesis: Mid-market industrial plasma power is not commoditized in China (imports persist per incumbent map) yet not export-controlled like advanced-node RF - a 2030 entrant can win on service + pulsing features while avoiding the semi-etch battleground; risk of Hengyunchang-class players moving down-market is the main clock.
demand_trigger_2030_2034: Named: Chinese PCB/advanced-packaging equipment upgrade cycle evidenced by recurring MOFCOM plasma-equipment tenders (L01-114, L01-039) and the AI-server PCB demand wave documented at Han's Laser (PCB equipment +50% in 2025, L12-037) - plasma cleaning scales with HDI/substrate volume through 2030-2034.
competition_outlook_2030: AE/MKS/Comet/TRUMPF premium imports (L01-040/049/050); Hengyunchang/Injet expanding from semi into industrial (L06-042/044); local low-end suppliers. Must win the middle on features-per-yuan and uptime service.
